description: A Class D conduct artifact whose stage 2 commit-chain preimage content, when canonicalized, exceeds the v0.2.0 D6 per-stage cap of 1,048,576 bytes (1 MiB) MUST receive CONDUCT_INVALID. Cap accounting is over the canonical JSON serialization (sorted keys, compact separators, UTF-8) per SPEC_LOCK_v0.2.0 §2.5.

input.claim: oversized preimage attached to a single commit stage

alternatives.summary: Leave D5 unbounded and let implementations choose their own cap.
alternatives.rejected_because: Implementation-defined caps fragment conformance and let producers attack the weakest verifier.

reasoning_trace.claim: D5 alone gives the attacker the choice between verifier OOM and verifier non-conformance.
A spec-level cap removes that choice without changing the preimage requirement.

recommended_action.summary: Add invariant D6 to v0.2.0 with per-stage 1 MiB and per-artifact 4 MiB caps.

challenge_surface.argument: 1 MiB may be too small for legitimate large-context governance artifacts; a future version may raise the cap.

success_signals: no implementer requests a cap increase within 6 months
failure_signals: multiple implementers report legitimate artifacts exceeding 1 MiB
a producer needs the cap raised to ship a real workflow

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.2.0 invariant D6 (SPEC_LOCK_v0.2.0 §2.5): a Class D commit-chain stage whose canonical JSON content exceeds 1,048,576 bytes MUST cause the artifact to be rejected as CONDUCT_INVALID. Stage 2 carries a single field whose canonical serialization is 1,048,614 bytes (24 bytes over the 1 MiB cap). Without D6, this artifact would force the verifier into the D5-vs-OOM dilemma described in threat T-D6-1. Exercises D6 directly; does not exercise D5 (preimage is present), CC1 (preimage is not null), or CC2 (depends_on is intact).
